reasons: Diggie reduces Carmilla's teamfight payoff by protecting allies during her linked-control window.
Carmilla needs enemies grouped and vulnerable; Diggie makes that window less reliable.

proof.summary: Carmilla wants to chain teamfight value through Curse of Blood, while Diggie can protect nearby teammates from key crowd-control timing.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves ultimate for Carmilla's real engage.
Allies stay close enough to benefit from the protection.
failureCases: Carmilla baits Diggie's ultimate before committing.
Diggie is caught before he can cast.
Teammates split too far away from Diggie.

reasons: Valir can push Carmilla away before she maintains close-range contact.
His zoning and slow pressure punish Carmilla when she must walk forward to create value.

proof.summary: Carmilla wants extended close-range fights, while Valir's knockback and fire-zone control can deny her entry path.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds knockback instead of using it only for poke.
The team kites backward after Carmilla commits.
failureCases: Carmilla reaches Valir from fog or flank.
Valir wastes knockback before Carmilla engages.
Carmilla's team layers engage after Valir's disengage is down.

reasons: Baxia reduces Carmilla's sustain value and can contest her close-range fighting pattern.
Carmilla's durability depends on repeated contact, so anti-regen pressure directly lowers her fight uptime.

proof.summary: Carmilla wants to survive through repeated close-range damage and healing, while Baxia applies shield and HP regeneration reduction through his skill interactions.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps contact with Carmilla or her frontline.
The team focuses Carmilla while her sustain is reduced.
failureCases: Baxia cannot reach Carmilla's actual fight area.
Carmilla disengages until anti-regen uptime ends.
Carmilla's team wins through burst before sustain reduction matters.

reasons: Karrie can punish Carmilla's tanky close-range style with repeated true damage based on max HP.
Carmilla has to enter Karrie's damage range to create value, which gives Karrie stacking opportunities.

proof.summary: Carmilla wants to absorb damage and stay in fights, while Karrie's Lightwheel Mark converts repeated hits into max-HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla is forced to walk through Karrie's attack range.
failureCases: Carmilla catches Karrie with linked CC before Karrie can stack hits.
Karrie is under-farmed or lacks attack-speed item timing.

reasons: Lunox can pressure Carmilla's durability through magic penetration and burst windows.
Carmilla is less comfortable when the enemy mage can damage frontline targets without relying on long basic-attack uptime.

proof.summary: Carmilla wants to outlast enemies in extended fights, while Lunox can use penetration-oriented magic damage to cut through her durability.
worksBestWhen: Lunox keeps distance and avoids being linked.
Carmilla's engage tools are already committed.
failureCases: Carmilla closes the gap before Lunox can cycle skills.
Lunox is forced to spend defensive tools early.
Carmilla's team follows up faster than Lunox can burst.
